nexus_prime.name: Central Exchange
nexus_prime.description: The largest trading floor in the Expanse. Three stories of holographic price boards and broker offices.
nexus_prime.flavor_text: The hum of commerce never stops here. Freight drones weave between the price boards overhead.

nexus_prime.name: Dockside Repair Bay
nexus_prime.description: Guild-certified repair bay where the diagnostics take longer than the repairs. Thorough, by-the-book, and they charge for every minute.
nexus_prime.flavor_text: Technicians in crisp blue coveralls poke at your hull with scanners that cost more than your cargo. They'll find problems you didn't know you had, then quote you for each one.

nexus_prime.name: The Aurelia's Rest
nexus_prime.description: A cantina in the old Aurelia Core. Where every faction rubs shoulders over cheap drinks.
nexus_prime.flavor_text: Union work songs leak from the back room. An Alliance farmer argues prices with a Guild broker. The bartender has heard it all.

nexus_prime.name: Meridian Shipworks
nexus_prime.description: The Guild's flagship shipyard. If the modification exists and the paperwork clears, they'll bolt it on.
nexus_prime.flavor_text: Holographic catalogs rotate slowly in glass cases. The prices all end in nines. A sales rep hovers the moment you slow down.

nexus_prime.name: Meridian Financial Exchange
nexus_prime.description: The Expanse's financial heart. Commodity futures, shipping contracts, and insurance policies change hands by the second.
nexus_prime.flavor_text: Numbers scroll across floor-to-ceiling displays. Fortunes are made and lost between lunch and dinner.

nexus_prime.name: Meridian Trade Office
nexus_prime.description: Fund a trade desk that profits from the constant flow of goods through Nexus Prime's markets.
nexus_prime.flavor_text: A small office wedged between two broker firms, its holographic sign already cycling projected returns. The location alone is worth the investment.

stellaris_port.name: The Grand Bazaar
stellaris_port.description: A three-level commercial complex of polished composite and soft lighting. High-end trade for high-end clients.
stellaris_port.flavor_text: The air smells faintly of imported perfume. A merchant displays fabrics that cost more than most ships.

stellaris_port.name: Premium Docking Services
stellaris_port.description: White-glove repair service. Your ship will look better than when you bought it.
stellaris_port.flavor_text: The repair techs wear uniforms nicer than most traders' dress clothes. The waiting lounge has real leather seats.

stellaris_port.name: The Gilt Nebula Lounge
stellaris_port.description: An upscale establishment where deals happen over drinks that cost a day's wages.
stellaris_port.flavor_text: Crystal glasses clink. Somebody laughs too loudly at a joke that wasn't funny. The wine list is longer than some trade contracts.

stellaris_port.name: Stellaris Outfitters
stellaris_port.description: Premium ship upgrades and modifications. If it exists, they stock it. For a price.
stellaris_port.flavor_text: Display models of high-end weapon systems rotate slowly behind glass cases.

stellaris_port.name: Stellaris Auction House
stellaris_port.description: Rare goods, antiquities, and items of uncertain provenance find new owners here. Cash only, no questions asked.
stellaris_port.flavor_text: A Core System painting goes for 50,000 credits. The buyer doesn't blink.

stellaris_port.name: Stellaris Brokerage
stellaris_port.description: Back a luxury goods brokerage catering to Stellaris Port's affluent clientele.
stellaris_port.flavor_text: The office features real wood furniture and a client list that reads like a who's who of the Expanse. Discretion is the primary service.

breakstone.name: Rock Haven Market
breakstone.description: Functional trading floor built into a converted cargo bay. No frills, fair prices.
breakstone.flavor_text: Ore dust hangs in the air. A miner haggles over drill bits with the same intensity she'd bring to a Council vote.

breakstone.name: Patch & Weld
breakstone.description: Union repair crews who learned their trade fixing mining rigs. Fast, cheap, and solid.
breakstone.flavor_text: The lead mechanic has more weld burns than hair. She'll fix your hull and tell you what you did wrong. Both free of charge.

breakstone.name: The Drift Bar
breakstone.description: A rowdy miners' bar where shift changes bring waves of thirsty regulars.
breakstone.flavor_text: Someone's scratched Union slogans into every table. The jukebox plays work songs. The beer is strong and the opinions stronger.

breakstone.name: Asteroid Belt Operations
breakstone.description: Rich asteroid fields surrounding the system. Common metals and iron for the taking.
breakstone.flavor_text: Mining skiffs drift between the rocks like fireflies. Breakstone's belts have been worked for a century and still haven't run dry.

breakstone.name: Union Dry Dock
breakstone.description: No-nonsense ship modifications. Union engineers build things to last.
breakstone.flavor_text: The upgrade catalog is shorter than the Guild's but everything on it has been field-tested by miners.

breakstone.name: The Deep Shafts
breakstone.description: The oldest mining tunnels in Breakstone. Section 3 preserves the cargo bay where the Uprising began.
breakstone.flavor_text: The walls here are scarred with a century of drill marks. A bronze plaque marks where Sora Takahashi spoke. Miners still leave flowers.

breakstone.name: Breakstone Mining Rig
breakstone.description: Fund an automated drilling rig in the asteroid fields. Returns paid in raw ore.
breakstone.flavor_text: A Union foreman shows you the rig schematics over a beer. Solid engineering, proven yields, and the miners' cooperative takes a fair cut.

iron_depths.name: Core Station Exchange
iron_depths.description: DCMC-operated trading post. Efficient, regulated, and focused on bulk mineral trade.
iron_depths.flavor_text: Digital manifests scroll across wall-mounted screens. Everything is weighed, assayed, and documented in triplicate.

iron_depths.name: DCMC Maintenance Bay
iron_depths.description: DCMC maintenance facility built to service heavy mining rigs. They'll work on your ship the same way, whether you like it or not.
iron_depths.flavor_text: The intake clerk assigns you a bay number without looking up. The queue board has twelve ships ahead of you. Nobody complains. Complaining doesn't move the queue.

iron_depths.name: The Quiet Shift
iron_depths.description: A subdued canteen where DCMC miners decompress between shifts. Quieter than Breakstone's bars.
iron_depths.flavor_text: Miners eat in near-silence, exhausted from twelve-hour shifts in the deep core. The coffee is industrial-strength.

iron_depths.name: Deep Core Operations
iron_depths.description: Dense, unstable asteroid clusters where the rare metals grow heavier the deeper you drill. The kind of place that pays well because it kills occasionally.
iron_depths.flavor_text: Gravitational anomalies twist your approach vector sideways. Veteran pilots read the field by instinct. Rookies read it by hull damage.

iron_depths.name: Restricted Sector 7
iron_depths.description: DCMC has quietly increased patrols around this sector. The official reason is 'geological instability.'
iron_depths.flavor_text: Warning beacons flash at the perimeter. Whatever's in there, DCMC doesn't want you seeing it.

iron_depths.name: Iron Depths Deep Drill
iron_depths.description: Invest in deep-core mining operations. High yield iron ore extraction.
iron_depths.flavor_text: The DCMC prospectus is clinical and precise. Core samples line the office wall like trophies, each one richer than the last.

forgeworks.name: Foundry Exchange
forgeworks.description: Industrial trading floor where raw materials become manufactured goods and everyone takes a cut.
forgeworks.flavor_text: The floor vibrates faintly from the smelters two levels below. Trade here smells like hot metal.

forgeworks.name: Syndicate Repair Dock
forgeworks.description: Independent repair facility run by the Forgeworks Industrial Syndicate. Competent and politically neutral.
forgeworks.flavor_text: The techs here fix Guild cruisers and Union haulers with equal proficiency. Credits are credits.

forgeworks.name: The Neutral Ground
forgeworks.description: A bar where Guild executives and Union foremen drink under the same roof. Arguments are frequent but knives stay sheathed.
forgeworks.flavor_text: A sign behind the bar reads: 'No politics before the third round.' Nobody obeys it, but the bartender appreciates the gesture.

forgeworks.name: Salvage Yards
forgeworks.description: Scrap metal, used parts, and refurbished equipment. The scrappier side of Forgeworks.
forgeworks.flavor_text: Mountains of hull plating and engine components, sorted by alloy and age. One person's wreck is another's inventory.

forgeworks.name: Main Foundry Refinery
forgeworks.description: Process raw materials in the Expanse's largest industrial refinery. Three shifts, never stops.
forgeworks.flavor_text: The glow of the smelters paints everything in amber. Ore goes in one end, alloys come out the other.

forgeworks.name: Orbital Shipyards
forgeworks.description: Where the Expanse's ships are built and modified. The Syndicate's pride and primary revenue source.
forgeworks.flavor_text: A half-finished freighter hangs in dry dock, its skeleton exposed like an anatomy lesson in steel.

forgeworks.name: Forgeworks Refining Contract
forgeworks.description: Lease capacity at a Union smelter. Returns paid in processed metals.
forgeworks.flavor_text: The Syndicate broker slides a contract across the table, still warm from the printer. The smelter runs three shifts and never sits idle.

axiom_labs.name: Research Supply Exchange
axiom_labs.description: Specialized market focused on medical tech, electronics, and research materials.
axiom_labs.flavor_text: Everything is labeled with chemical formulas and batch numbers. The traders here have doctorates.

axiom_labs.name: Engineering Department Bay
axiom_labs.description: Collective engineers who treat ship repair like a research problem. Thorough but methodical.
axiom_labs.flavor_text: They'll scan your hull with instruments that cost more than your ship, then explain exactly what's wrong in terms you won't understand.

axiom_labs.name: The Faculty Lounge
axiom_labs.description: Where Axiom's researchers unwind. Conversations tend toward the technical even after hours.
axiom_labs.flavor_text: A biologist and a physicist argue about quantum entanglement. The bartender, who has a master's degree, pours drinks and corrects their math.

axiom_labs.name: Materials Science Lab
axiom_labs.description: Advanced refining processes developed by Collective researchers. Precision over volume.
axiom_labs.flavor_text: Centrifuges hum in clean rooms. The yield is lower than Forgeworks, but the purity is unmatched.

axiom_labs.name: Engineering Workshop
axiom_labs.description: Ship modifications designed by people who publish papers about them. The alloys are lighter, the circuits are faster, and the invoices are eye-watering.
axiom_labs.flavor_text: A researcher explains the molecular bonding process for your new hull plating. You nod politely. The performance specs, at least, speak for themselves.

axiom_labs.name: Okafor Institute Medical Wing
axiom_labs.description: The Collective's premier medical facility. Dr. Okafor's legacy: 'Knowledge that does not heal is knowledge wasted.'
axiom_labs.flavor_text: A bronze plaque gleams beside the entrance. Inside, researchers work on breakthroughs that could change everything, if the patents don't bury them first.

axiom_labs.name: Axiom Research Grant
axiom_labs.description: Fund a research initiative that pays dividends from patent licensing.
axiom_labs.flavor_text: The grant proposal is dense with jargon but the licensing projections are clear. Collective research has a way of finding commercial applications.

nova_research.name: Nova Trading Terminal
nova_research.description: Restricted trading access. High-tech goods at premium prices, with export controls on sensitive items.
nova_research.flavor_text: The manifest forms require three signatures and a non-disclosure agreement. Welcome to Nova.

nova_research.name: NAS Technical Services
nova_research.description: Corporate repair facility. Professional, expensive, and they'll scan your ship while they fix it.
nova_research.flavor_text: The techs are polite and efficient. You try not to think about what their diagnostic scanners are recording.

nova_research.name: The Clean Room
nova_research.description: A sterile, quiet bar where NAS employees speak in careful euphemisms about their work.
nova_research.flavor_text: The drinks are expensive and the conversations are guarded. Everyone here has signed something they can't talk about.

nova_research.name: Advanced Processing Lab
nova_research.description: Proprietary refining processes that produce the sector's most advanced materials. Results classified.
nova_research.flavor_text: The yields here defy published theoretical limits. NAS isn't sharing how.

nova_research.name: NAS Systems Integration
nova_research.description: Ship systems that don't appear in any commercial database. The installation techs have clearances higher than most station commanders.
nova_research.flavor_text: The catalog is a locked terminal. You scroll past weapons systems with redacted names and shield configurations labeled 'experimental.' The tech watches what you linger on.

nova_research.name: Restricted Research Wing
nova_research.description: Entire sections of Nova are off-limits. Armed guards, biometric locks, and persistent rumors about what's inside.
nova_research.flavor_text: A researcher hurries past with a sealed container. She doesn't make eye contact. The guard watches you watch her.

nova_research.name: Nova Applied Sciences Fund
nova_research.description: Support cutting-edge research with commercial applications. Returns paid in licensing fees.
nova_research.flavor_text: The NAS investment officer speaks in carefully hedged language. The returns column, however, speaks for itself.

havens_rest.name: Harvest Market
havens_rest.description: An open market where farmers sell directly to traders. Fresh bread competes with engine exhaust.
havens_rest.flavor_text: A farmer samples her own cheese for a skeptical trader. Three stalls down, someone is selling honey from actual bees.

havens_rest.name: Community Repair Cooperative
havens_rest.description: Volunteer mechanics and shared tools. They'll fix what they can and teach you to fix the rest.
havens_rest.flavor_text: A teenager with grease-stained hands explains hull patching technique with the confidence of someone twice her age.

havens_rest.name: The Long Table
havens_rest.description: More community kitchen than cantina. Everyone eats together. Strangers become regulars fast.
havens_rest.flavor_text: The soup is homemade and the bread is still warm. An old farmer tells a story about the first harvest. Nobody rushes.

havens_rest.name: Frontier Outfitters
havens_rest.description: Alliance engineers who learned their trade keeping colony ships flying past their expiration date. Nothing fancy, everything works.
havens_rest.flavor_text: The catalog is hand-written on a whiteboard in three colors of marker. The engineer crosses things out and adds them back as stock changes. She apologizes for the mess. The work, however, is spotless.

havens_rest.name: Alliance Congress Hall
havens_rest.description: Where the Annual Congress meets. Delegates from every Alliance community come to argue, negotiate, and share meals.
havens_rest.flavor_text: The walls are covered in banners from every Alliance settlement. The table seats two hundred. The arguments can last longer than the dinners.

havens_rest.name: Haven's Rest Hydroponics
havens_rest.description: Fund a hydroponic farming operation in a frontier community. Steady food production.
havens_rest.flavor_text: The cooperative elder walks you through the greenhouse plans, pointing out each growing rack with quiet pride. Frontier folk repay their debts.

verdant.name: Agri-Hub Exchange
verdant.description: Distribution center connecting surface farms to interstellar trade. Specialty crops at premium prices.
verdant.flavor_text: Crates of organic produce line the loading bays. The quality labels here actually mean something.

verdant.name: Farmstead Mechanics
verdant.description: Agricultural engineers who fix everything from irrigation pumps to starship hulls. Practical and affordable.
verdant.flavor_text: The mechanic adjusts her approach from tractor to spacecraft without missing a beat. A hull is a hull.

verdant.name: The Greenhouse
verdant.description: A cantina surrounded by actual growing plants. The drinks feature locally-grown ingredients.
verdant.flavor_text: Vines climb the walls and real sunlight filters through the dome. The cider is made from Verdant apples. It's unreasonably good.

verdant.name: Verdant Docking Services
verdant.description: Practical ship modifications from people who've actually flown the routes. Cargo hauling and fuel efficiency, nothing more.
verdant.flavor_text: The mechanic tests your thruster alignment by ear before checking the instruments. She recommends the cheaper option. It's also the better one.

verdant.name: Mayors' Council Chamber
verdant.description: The most organized governing body in the Alliance. Where Verdant's uncomfortable success gets debated.
verdant.flavor_text: Minutes from the last session are posted on the wall. The agenda item 'modernization proposal' has been tabled for the fourth time.

verdant.name: Verdant Agricultural Co-op
verdant.description: Buy shares in a farming cooperative. Returns paid in food supplies.
verdant.flavor_text: The co-op's ledger is handwritten and meticulous. Verdant soil yields three harvests a year, and the farmers have never missed a dividend.

crimson_reach.name: Wrecker's Bazaar
crimson_reach.description: Salvaged parts, refurbished electronics, and goods of ambiguous origin. Questions aren't welcome and receipts don't exist.
crimson_reach.flavor_text: A market that doesn't recognize the concept of contraband. If it's on the table, it's for sale.

crimson_reach.name: Jury-Rig Junction
crimson_reach.description: They'll fix your ship with whatever's on hand. Unconventional methods, surprisingly effective results.
crimson_reach.flavor_text: The mechanic welds a hull plate that clearly came from a different ship. It fits. Don't ask how.

crimson_reach.name: The Rust Bucket
crimson_reach.description: A salvager's bar built from three different ships welded together. The drinks are strong and the stories are stronger.
crimson_reach.flavor_text: Someone is arm-wrestling for salvage rights. The bartender, who is also the bouncer, watches with professional disinterest.

crimson_reach.name: Debris Field Operations
crimson_reach.description: The richest salvage grounds in the Expanse. Old battles and abandoned stations provide endless material.
crimson_reach.flavor_text: Wrecker crews launch at dawn and return at dusk with holds full of history. One person's wreck is another's livelihood.

crimson_reach.name: Wrecker's Guild Hall
crimson_reach.description: The only structure resembling authority in Crimson Reach. Safety standards, dispute mediation, and a shared database of derelict locations.
crimson_reach.flavor_text: Malia Torres's reputation keeps the peace better than any law could. The derelict map on the wall is worth more than most ships.

crimson_reach.name: Crimson Reach Salvage Op
crimson_reach.description: Sponsor a salvage crew working the debris fields. Returns paid in scrap metal.
crimson_reach.flavor_text: The crew captain spreads a star chart across the bar, circling derelict sites with a grease pencil. Every wreck out there is money waiting to be claimed.

crimson_reach.name: The Reach Floor
crimson_reach.description: An auction floor run out of a converted shipyard bay. The Wreckers' Guild gates entry; Vex Tarn runs the room. Crates come off the trucks and onto the block.
crimson_reach.flavor_text: No catalogue. No provenance. The reserve is the reserve.

the_fulcrum.name: Seized Supply Cache
the_fulcrum.description: Military supply crates stacked in a cargo hold. Not a market in any real sense, but there are goods here that could be useful.
the_fulcrum.flavor_text: Everything is stamped with Guild logistics codes. Most of it is weapons components, but there are rations and fuel cells mixed in.

the_fulcrum.name: Maintenance Docking Port
the_fulcrum.description: A neglected docking port on the station's lower ring. Colonial-era construction, never updated. The only approach that avoids the automated defense perimeter.
the_fulcrum.flavor_text: Emergency lighting flickers in the airlock. The station hums with power that has no business being here.

the_fulcrum.name: Emergency Repair Bay
the_fulcrum.description: A functional repair bay built into the original colonial infrastructure. Basic but operational.
the_fulcrum.flavor_text: Spare parts are scattered across workbenches. Everything here has been stripped from Guild military vessels.

the_fulcrum.name: Assembly Core
the_fulcrum.description: The heart of The Fulcrum. Massive gravitational lensing arrays hang in zero-gravity assembly cradles, their curved surfaces gleaming under industrial floodlights. A warp gate frame dominates the far wall.
the_fulcrum.flavor_text: The scale is breathtaking. Each array is larger than your ship. And the warp gate behind them glows with a faint blue light, already powered, already waiting.
